agent_notes: Hackney is unusually strong for Show & Tell Drinks and Market Days, but Creative Walks are constrained by the PAINTS v6 safety list: Hackney Marshes is removed, Clissold Park is paused, and Abney Park is not permitted.
Museum & Gallery picks lean toward Hoxton/Shoreditch/Hackney Wick because several central Hackney civic venues are closed or not running a current art/craft exhibition on 2026-07-04.
Many excellent Hackney bookshops do not have on-site or literally next-door independent coffee; those are rejected rather than padded.
Opening hours and walk-in policies are live-source researched, but all 8-12 group walk-ins remain UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Wed 17:00-23:00, Thu 17:00-00:00, Fri 17:00-01:00, Sat 14:00-01:00, Sun 14:00-23:00. Bank holiday and private-hire status UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bar format suggests walk-ins, but group 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable only at early or Sunday slots with a host call.
venues.capacity_notes: Compact converted pub wine bar; use early evening before peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of east London's defining natural-wine rooms: small, atmospheric, and conversational rather than flashy. The daily-changing list gives every attendee something to bring to Show & Tell. It has enough cultural validation to feel special without becoming a hotel-lobby experience.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded by Michael and Charlotte Sager-Wilde after a pop-up, it helped push London's modern wine-bar scene from Hackney Road.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Low-intervention and classic wines by the glass and bottle; small plates/snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Hackney Road/Hoxton is busy and transport-rich; route guests to Hoxton Overground or buses after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room and reputation; host should call about any 8-12 group.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Thu 16:00-00:00, Fri 16:00-01:00, Sat 12:00-01:00, Sun 12:00-00:00. Bank holiday/private hire UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; walk-ins for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for 8-12 than tiny wine shops if contacted early; unverified.
venues.capacity_notes: Cocktail bar with private-hire enquiries; PAINTS should avoid Friday late peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Equal Parts gives a sharper cocktail-led version of the Hackney Road wine-bar story. It has vinyl, aperitivo energy, and a founder link to Sager + Wilde, so it is recognisable but not generic. The room's Hopper-ish minimalism suits visual icebreakers.
venues.anecdote_story: Michael Sager opened it as a Sager + Wilde sibling, with press noting an Edward Hopper/Nighthawks influence.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, low-intervention wine, beer and bar snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Same Hackney Road corridor as Sager + Wilde; use main-road routes to Hoxton.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strong industry buzz can make prime evenings too busy for a spontaneous group.

venues.opening_hours: Official restaurant page lists dinner Tue-Sat from 18:00 and Saturday lunch from 12:00. SevenRooms says reservations for groups of 7-12 are available. Walk-in availability and bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations are the operational norm; SquareMeal says some walk-in seats may be held when possible. Tier B because group walk-ins are fragile.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if PAINTS books La Cachette or equivalent group table; not robust as pure walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Restaurant/cellar with group dining path; expensive for casual drinks.
venues.why_fits_paints: Planque is visually and gastronomically strong: a Haggerston railway-arch cellar with serious wine culture. It gives PAINTS a more grown-up, design-literate table. The booking dependency keeps it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Planque operates as a restaurant and cellar, with a reputation for deep wine culture in Acton Mews.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine-led restaurant menu, cellar bottles and seasonal cooking.
venues.safety_notes: Acton Mews is tucked away; host should route attendees directly from Haggerston Overground.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation-led and £££; not suitable for a true unbooked PAINTS drop-in.

venues.opening_hours: OpenTable lists Mon-Thu 17:00-00:00, Fri-Sat 14:00-00:00, Sun 14:00-23:00; dinner Mon-Sat 18:00-22:30, Sun 18:00-21:30. Bank holiday/private hire UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says walk-ins always welcome; reservations up to 8. Group 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8 only by booking; 9-12 likely requires direct contact.
venues.capacity_notes: Stylish neighbourhood wine bar/restaurant in a former pub; may be loud later.
venues.why_fits_paints: Goodbye Horses is a crafted De Beauvoir room with natural wine, folk-art details and a conversation-starting sound/design identity. It is polished but still neighbourhood-sized. The official walk-in language helps, though group size must be checked.
venues.anecdote_story: Yatzer and World of Interiors trace the project to Alex Young and George de Vos, with sister coffee and ice-cream concepts nearby.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine, sharing plates, coffee at sister Day Trip nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Leafy residential streets are calm but quieter after dark; route guests to Essex Road/Canonbury or Dalston Junction in pairs.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservations cap at 8 and prices are higher than PAINTS default.

venues.opening_hours: The Nudge lists Tue 12:00-18:00 and Wed-Sat 12:00-late; Instagram confirms walk-ins only and current activity. Exact 2026 weekly hours/bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: No reservations / walk-ins only per SquareMeal and Parched; group 8-12 acceptance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Communal tables make it plausible early; not guaranteed at peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Small wine bar/shop with communal tables and a living-room feel.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 17:00-19:00; Saturday early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: Dan's is casual enough for strangers and serious enough for wine people. The walk-ins-only policy fits PAINTS better than reservation-first restaurants. Dalston's wine-bar density gives good contingency.
venues.anecdote_story: Parched describes Dan's as the result of 18 months of work and a physical space to drink the wines they sell.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine shop/bar, many bottles, by-the-glass pours and snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Tottenham Road/Kingsland Road has high footfall; direct departures to Dalston Junction.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Walk-ins only means no guaranteed table for 8-12 at peak.

venues.opening_hours: TableAgent lists Thu-Sat style evening hours including 17:00-23:00; Facebook notes walk-ins only for events. Exact 2026 hours/bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins only signal found on Facebook; 8-12 group suitability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small bar/shop; use only early and with host check.
venues.capacity_notes: Cosy Austrian/natural wine shop-bar, likely tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: Newcomer adds a distinctive Austrian-wine angle to Dalston rather than another generic bottle shop. It is intimate and specific, with a nice Show & Tell hook around place, grape and importer taste. Operationally it is best as a smaller group or backup.
venues.anecdote_story: Time Out notes the venue's claim to stock London's largest selection of Austrian wine and beer.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Austrian and low-intervention wines, bottle-shop browsing, light food/snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Dalston Lane is active and close to Dalston Junction; avoid late loitering.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small review footprint and group capacity uncertainty.

venues.opening_hours: Resident Advisor describes it as a Hackney bar/restaurant open six days a week for dinner service; official site is live but exact hours are not fully exposed in search result. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations exist; bar/a-la-carte may be possible but group 8-12 walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Tier B for operational fragility.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible with booking; risky as walk-in due music/dinner format.
venues.capacity_notes: Listening bar and restaurant; later music may overwhelm discussion.
venues.best_time_slot: Early dinner/bar opening before DJ volume rises.
venues.why_fits_paints: The music-listening culture and natural-wine/Japanese-food mix give Brilliant Corners a real story. It is visually and culturally strong, but not quiet. PAINTS should treat it as a high-energy backup rather than a default conversation room.
venues.anecdote_story: The Spaces called it a pioneer of London's listening-bar renaissance and noted its 10th anniversary.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine, Japanese/Asian food, audiophile sound system and DJ programming.
venues.safety_notes: Kingsland Road is high-footfall and well served; direct attendees to Dalston Junction.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Noise and reservation model can undermine 2-hour stranger conversation.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Fri 16:00-23:00, Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; CAMRA lists similar hours. Bank holiday/private hire UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Neighbourhood pub walk-ins implied; explicit 8-12 group walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable in garden or early pub hours; host call needed.
venues.capacity_notes: Victorian pub with garden, fireplaces and pizza/beer/wine offer.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the rare pub where the story is as strong as the room: a community-saved Victorian local. It supports easy conversation better than many fashionable wine bars. It is less natural-wine coded, but the history makes it PAINTS-worthy.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub became Hackney's first Asset of Community Value and reopened after a campaign to save it from flat conversion.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, wine, pub drinks, Yard Sale/pizza-friendly food culture, garden.
venues.safety_notes: Residential route; use Hackney Central/Homerton main-road approaches after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a specialist wine bar; group seating still unconfirmed.

venues.opening_hours: SquareMeal lists Mon-Fri 16:30-23:00, Fri to midnight, Sat-Sun noon-midnight with Sun to 22:30; Dough Hands lists food Mon-Fri 16:30-21:00, Sat 12:00-21:00, Sun 12:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram mentions walk-ins; 8-12 group seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable in off-peak pub/garden hours.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy pub with Dough Hands pizza; stronger for casual pub table than quiet deep conversation.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Spurstowe is a textured local with enough food and garden energy for a relaxed group. Dough Hands gives it a current cultural-food hook. It is Tier B because the PAINTS story is thinner than Chesham or Sager.
venues.anecdote_story: Recent press frames it as a Hackney pub revived by the Dough Hands residency and local mood.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, wine list, Dough Hands pizza.
venues.safety_notes: Greenwood Road is residential but near main routes; direct guests to Hackney Downs/Central.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tripadvisor signal is mixed; verify recent Google sentiment before event.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram states open every day from 12; official site is active with booking/food pages. Exact closing, bank holiday and private-hire status UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Booking available; walk-in policy for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible in pub area off-peak; host should call.
venues.capacity_notes: Backstreet pub with food and cocktails; avoid Sunday roast peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper East End pub with polished food, cocktails and a visually strong Victorian-corner identity. It is familiar enough to put strangers at ease but not flat. The location balances the list away from the Hoxton/Dalston cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: London Pub Explorer notes care taken to preserve the pub's link to its past after reopening.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, cocktails, food and Sunday roast.
venues.safety_notes: Residential backstreets near London Fields; route guests to London Fields or Hackney Central after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Sunday roast and dinner peaks may make 8-12 seating difficult.

venues.opening_hours: Official BMTRA site lists market 09:00-17:00 Saturday and 10:00-16:00 Sunday; Hackney Council lists Saturday 09:00-17:00 and Sunday 10:00-17:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good outdoors; meet before peak crowd.
venues.capacity_notes: Crowded at noon; best as split browsing pairs with re-gather points.
venues.why_fits_paints: Broadway is obvious, but it still clears because it has producer food, street texture, and independent shops on every side. PAINTS should frame it through makers rather than tourist grazing. It pairs easily with Netil or London Fields edge without using London Fields as a Creative Walk.
venues.anecdote_story: A working Victorian market street, with the modern weekend market selling fresh produce, street food, clothing, arts and crafts.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, produce, fashion, flowers, books/shops nearby.
venues.safety_notes: High footfall and good daytime safety; avoid canal towpath routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Crowding and obviousness; do not schedule at peak Saturday lunch.

venues.opening_hours: Eat Work Art lists Mon/Tue/Sun 09:00-18:00 and Wed-Sat 09:00-22:00, noting trader hours vary; Visit London confirms similar. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; SquareMeal says reservations not required. Meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for outdoor browsing and food ordering; covered options vary by trader.
venues.capacity_notes: Small creative market; keep group mobile rather than seated for two hours.
venues.why_fits_paints: Netil is more compact and creative-worker coded than Broadway, with food traders, retail and Netil Radio texture. It has enough atmosphere without forcing a single table. It works well as a lower-pressure market day.
venues.anecdote_story: Eat Work Art frames it as a creative community market tied to Hackney's workspace/studio ecosystem.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, retail pop-ups, market stalls, radio/music ambience.
venues.safety_notes: Strong daytime footfall near Broadway Market; use main streets rather than isolated canal stretches.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Trader-by-trader hours vary; host should verify the exact traders for event day.

venues.opening_hours: Hackney Council news says Chatsworth Road Market runs every Sunday 10:00-17:00; Instagram lists Sunday from 10:00-17:00. Bank holiday/holiday exceptions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good outdoor capacity; quieter than Broadway.
venues.capacity_notes: Pedestrianised Sunday market zone; best as wandering format.
venues.why_fits_paints: Chatsworth Road feels local and lived-in rather than destination-polished. The new Sunday pedestrianisation gives it safer browsing and more linger time. It is one of Hackney's best market-day fits when Broadway feels too obvious.
venues.anecdote_story: Hackney Council describes the car-free Sunday setup as a way to support the road's long market history and community feel.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food, produce, vintage, crafts and local shops/cafes.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Sunday pedestrianisation improves safety; use Clapton/Homerton routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/walk combination; verify exact route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposure and variable trader mix.

venues.opening_hours: Hackney Council lists Mon-Sat 09:30-16:00 and closed on public/bank holidays except Good Friday; Hackney Council news says Sunday Collective now runs Sundays 10:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing but narrow/crowded; host should set clear meeting points.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy working market with strong food/cultural identity; less polished than PAINTS default.
venues.why_fits_paints: Ridley Road is culturally deep, vivid and historically important. It is Tier B because operational comfort and crowd management are harder for strangers than at Broadway/Chatsworth. The story is excellent if the host can hold the group well.
venues.anecdote_story: London Museum links the market to anti-fascist and anti-gentrification histories, with roots in the 1880s.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fruit, vegetables, Caribbean/African/Asian groceries, clothing, household goods and Sunday Collective stalls.
venues.safety_notes: High footfall but busy/narrow; daytime only and clear arrival/departure via Dalston Kingsland.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/tube combination; verify on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Recent press mentions trader/regeneration and crime concerns; host should check local conditions before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Hackney Council lists Saturday 09:00-16:00 and closed on public/bank holidays except Good Friday; Visit London lists Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00 and Sat 09:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible, but it is a working market rather than a designed leisure market.
venues.capacity_notes: Best as short browse plus nearby cafe/gallery, not a full 3-hour market hang.
venues.why_fits_paints: Hoxton Street is less glossy than Broadway and has a long East End street-market history. It works for PAINTS if framed through local texture and makers, not as a premium food market. Tier B reflects fewer aesthetic/producer signals.
venues.anecdote_story: OpenMarkets describes Hoxton as Hackney's oldest street market, established in 1687.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street goods, food stalls, local retail, occasional youth/maker market programming.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime main-street route is acceptable; avoid late-night market framing.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min transit; verify on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less curated than PAINTS ideal; check trader presence before event.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists every Saturday 10:00-14:30 at St Paul's Church, Stoke Newington N16 7UE. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers' market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good outdoor courtyard format, especially earlier.
venues.capacity_notes: Shorter hours; plan a 2-hour event, not full afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the most values-aligned Hackney market: organic, local, producer-led and genuinely community-rooted. The food-system story gives hosts more substance than a generic brunch wander. It is a quiet PAINTS standout.
venues.anecdote_story: Growing Communities calls it the UK's only all-organic farmers' market and links it to small sustainable farms mostly within 60 miles of London.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Organic produce, bread, coffee/cake, gozleme and weekly producer stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime churchyard/High Street area; route via Stoke Newington Church Street and main bus corridors.
venues.backup_venue: Hackney Flea Market at Abney Public Hall
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk when Hackney Flea is running; otherwise no same-day market backup within 15 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Only Saturdays until 14:30; weather and weekly stall list should be checked.

venues.venue_name: Hackney Flea Market at Abney Public Hall
venues.opening_hours: Official site shows future Stoke Newington/Hackney Flea dates such as 17-18 Oct 2026, 10:00-17:00; Instagram showed May 2026 weekend at Abney Hall. Date-specific operation required.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access/ticketed market depending date; group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing, but event dates are irregular.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor/community hall vintage market; manage group through stalls in pairs.
venues.why_fits_paints: The flea format gives PAINTS the strongest Show & Tell object prompts in the market category. It is full of salvaged, vintage and handmade goods. Tier B because it is periodic rather than weekly.
venues.anecdote_story: Bustler says Hackney Flea began as a pop-up in 2013 to offer vintage, salvage and artisan wares.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Vintage clothing, homewares, records, salvage, textiles and handmade objects.
venues.safety_notes: Use Stoke Newington Church Street daytime; do not route through Abney Park as a walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk if both operate same Saturday; otherwise no qualifying market backup within 15 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Irregular dates; verify before listing any event.

venues.venue_name: Hertford Union Canal / Victoria Park Edge from Hackney Wick
venues.opening_hours: Open-access daytime route only. PAINTS v6 permits Hertford Union Canal only as a Victoria Park edge pairing; Victoria Park is on the Safety-First Shortlist.
venues.walk_in_policy: N/A for open-access walk; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a daytime hosted walk only; keep to high-footfall edges and daylight.
venues.capacity_notes: 3-4 stops: Hackney Wick station/studio yards, canal edge, Victoria Park edge, Olympic Park sightline. Do not extend onto Lee Navigation or Hackney Marshes.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the only Hackney-adjacent Creative Walk that can be defended under v6 without breaking the park rules. It combines industrial/studio Hackney Wick texture with the approved Victoria Park edge. It is Tier B because the qualifying park is just outside the borough and the canal permission is narrow.
venues.anecdote_story: The walk can tell the story of Hackney Wick's post-industrial studio/warehouse landscape, the Hertford Union Canal, and Victoria Park's approved safety status.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Canal infrastructure, Hackney Wick studios/warehouses, Victoria Park edge, public art/street art sightlines.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only. Victoria Park is Safety-First Shortlist; do not route through Hackney Marshes, Lee Navigation extended, Abney Park or Clissold Park.
venues.backup_venue: Stour Space / Hackney Wick cafe-gallery area
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Hackney Wick station; host must confirm current opening.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tightest caveat in this file: Hackney itself lacks an approved park route, so this is a border/edge route.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sun and Bank Holiday Mondays 10:00-17:00, free entry, no booking required.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says no booking is required; 8-12 group visit is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for self-guided group with meeting point; avoid school/group peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum scale supports 8-12; cafe/garden meeting needs confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Rooms Through Time/Home Galleries give the category a very PAINTS conversation engine: everyone has a home story. It is operationally robust and free. The exhibition-specific hook is less current-art driven than commercial galleries, but the institution is perfect for matched strangers.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum occupies former almshouses and reopened after an £18.1m redevelopment that expanded gallery space.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rooms Through Time, Home Galleries, domestic objects, gardens and changing displays.
venues.safety_notes: Directly opposite Hoxton Overground with busy Kingsland Road frontage.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Check current temporary exhibitions if PAINTS wants a press-timed art hook.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram lists current exhibition Okiki Akinfe 'RM9 5AN' until 8 Aug 2026 and open Wed-Sat 12:00-18:00; access page gives nearby public facility hours. Bank holiday/private event UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public gallery; 8-12 group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for compact gallery visit if quiet; call before bringing a group.
venues.capacity_notes: Small contemporary gallery; pair with Hoxton Street or Museum of the Home.
venues.why_fits_paints: PEER is small, public-facing and artist-led enough to feel discovered. The current Okiki Akinfe show gives a live 2026 exhibition hook. It suits slow looking and discussion better than a blockbuster.
venues.anecdote_story: PEER is embedded on Hoxton Street and includes Khadija's Garden, giving a civic/gallery texture beyond white-cube viewing.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current contemporary exhibition: Okiki Akinfe 'RM9 5AN' until 8 Aug 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Hoxton Street is active by day; use main route from Hoxton/Old Street.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small space; exhibition changeover could close galleries.

venues.opening_hours: New Exhibitions lists Tue-Sat 11:00-18:00; Instagram says open Mon-Sat 10:00-17:00 and Sun 12:00-17:00 for a current context. Host must confirm exact Kingsland Road hours and event-date exhibition.
venues.walk_in_policy: Commercial gallery walk-ins normally accepted during public hours; group 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if no private view/install; call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery scale is manageable for 8-12; no long dwell seating.
venues.why_fits_paints: Flowers gives Hackney a proper contemporary-gallery anchor with enough scale for group looking. The June/July climate-week programming and Kingsland Road location make it easy to pair. It is polished without requiring a ticketed museum plan.
venues.anecdote_story: Flowers is a long-running contemporary gallery with a Kingsland Road space and a programme including Edward Burtynsky/landscape-related 2026 activity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary exhibitions; source pages reference June/July 2026 Flowers programming and upcoming exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Hoxton Overground and Museum of the Home on busy Kingsland Road.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Installation periods may close; verify before event.

venues.opening_hours: Official exhibitions page lists Ally Fallon, 'At the still point of the turning world', 4 Jun-17 Jul 2026, London. Public hours for exact date are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Commercial gallery public viewing likely during opening hours; group 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if no private appointment/install conflict.
venues.capacity_notes: Contemporary gallery; pair with nearby Autograph or Flowers.
venues.why_fits_paints: Hales has a current painting-led 2026 exhibition, which directly supports Museum & Gallery Days. The show is current enough for a live cultural hook. It is slightly outside core Hackney but useful for the Shoreditch/Hoxton cultural cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: Hales has a long international programme in London and New York; the 2026 Ally Fallon show is the artist's debut solo exhibition with the gallery.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ally Fallon, 'At the still point of the turning world', 4 Jun-17 Jul 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Shoreditch High Street/Bethnal Green Road has high footfall; direct station routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline district geography and unverified public hours.

venues.venue_name: The Viktor Wynd Museum of Curiosities, Fine Art & UnNatural History
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Fri 15:00-22:00 and Sat-Sun 12:00-22:00. Ticket/walk-in availability and private events UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Visit London notes Tuesday walk-in customer tickets; normal 8-12 admission/walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally possible but content is polarising; host must pre-screen tone.
venues.capacity_notes: Small curiosity museum and bar; not suitable for sensitive groups.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is undeniably characterful and object-rich, with enough oddity for immediate conversation. The fit is weaker for PAINTS warmth because some material may be macabre or uncomfortable. Tier B: brilliant for the right host, wrong for a broad launch default.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum grew from Viktor Wynd/The Last Tuesday Society and includes curiosities, fine art, natural history and surreal/outsider material.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Curiosities, taxidermy, surreal/fine art material, natural history oddities and museum bar.
venues.safety_notes: Mare Street/Cambridge Heath corridor is active; route via main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Macabre content and ticketing make it less universally safe for strangers.

venues.opening_hours: Official exhibitions page lists gallery hours Tue 11:00-18:00, Wed 11:00-18:00, Thu 11:00-21:00, Fri 11:00-18:00, Sat 12:00-18:00, Sun/Mon closed. Current exhibition on exact date UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery during opening hours; 8-12 group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for small group looking if no private event.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery scale supports 8-12; pair with Hoxton/PEER.
venues.why_fits_paints: Autograph adds a rigorous photography, identity and social-history lens to the Hackney/Shoreditch gallery set. It gives PAINTS substance, not just pretty walls. The district line is a caveat but it is within the cultural radius.
venues.anecdote_story: Whitechapel Gallery describes Autograph as a charity founded in 1988 advocating inclusion of historically marginalised photographic practices.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Photography/film exhibitions around cultural identity, race, representation and human rights.
venues.safety_notes: Rivington Place/Shoreditch has high footfall; use Shoreditch High Street routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline Hackney geography and exhibition-changeover risk.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Hackney Wick / Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
venues.opening_hours: Official V&A East page lists daily 10:00-18:00, Thursday and Saturday 10:00-22:00, closed 24-26 Dec; admission free with some paid exhibitions/events.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free admission but official site warns there may be a queue; 8-12 group entry is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good museum scale; queue risk must be managed.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum suited to 8-12; use a fixed meeting point.
venues.why_fits_paints: Although technically an Olympic Park/Hackney Wick edge pick, V&A East gives PAINTS a major new cultural anchor with current press attention. It is visually ambitious and operationally robust. The obviousness penalty is real, but the launch-year energy is useful.
venues.anecdote_story: Guardian coverage frames its inaugural exhibition as a large-scale story of Black British music, with more than 200 objects.
venues.serve_or_on_view: V&A East displays and inaugural/current exhibitions including Black British music material per 2026 press.
venues.safety_notes: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park is on PAINTS Premium Launch Parks; use main lit routes and Hackney Wick/Stratford transit.
venues.backup_venue: Stour Space / Hackney Wick gallery-cafe area
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline district and queue risk; host should verify paid/free exhibition availability.

venues.opening_hours: Official Find Us page lists Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00 and Sun 12:00-18:00. Easter 2026 hours varied per Facebook. Cafe/current coffee and group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: In-person browsing accepted; group 8-12 and cafe seating are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for browsing plus nearby cafe; 2-hour seated stay unverified.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop with indie press/zine focus; cafe claim is secondary-sourced and must be checked.
venues.why_fits_paints: Burley Fisher has the strongest literary identity in Hackney: indie presses, zines, events and proper bookseller taste. It is not a generic coffee shop with a shelf. The coffee/cafe rule needs host confirmation, but the bookshop itself is excellent.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says it was London's bookshop of the year in 2021 and 2022; The Bookseller reported it opened with an independent-press focus and a coffee-shop element.
venues.serve_or_on_view: New and second-hand books, indie presses, zines and guest bookseller lists.
venues.safety_notes: Kingsland Road/Haggerston is active; route via Haggerston Overground.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: On-site cafe/seating must be verified; otherwise demote or pair with literal next-door coffee only if compliant.

venues.opening_hours: Bookshop Crawl lists Mon-Fri and Sun 11:00-18:00, Sat 10:00-18:00; Instagram confirms current activity and bank-holiday exceptions. Cafe/group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: In-person browsing accepted; 8-12 group and coffee seating policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing; 2-hour seated Books & Coffee format likely fragile.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent bookshop; basement/access limits noted by Bookshop Crawl.
venues.why_fits_paints: Pages is deeply Hackney and personally curated, with strong bookseller warmth. It becomes Tier B only because the coffee/seating evidence is weaker than Burley Fisher or Common Press. If the host confirms a literal cafe setup, it could rise.
venues.anecdote_story: If Lost Start Here calls it a bright blue beacon on Lower Clapton Road and highlights its in-person browsing and ordering service.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent bookshop shelves, ordering, gift wrapping and local literary identity.
venues.safety_notes: Lower Clapton Road is a main corridor; use daytime or early evening.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk to Chatsworth Road; no confirmed same-category bookshop-cafe backup within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee requirement is the binding constraint; host must confirm before using.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Dalston/North address 97 Stoke Newington Road, open Wed-Sun 11:00-17:00; Time Out reports the new site opened with the same hours and may stay open later for events.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public bookshop/cafe/community space; 8-12 group walk-in seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable only off-peak or with heads-up.
venues.capacity_notes: Newer branch; seating/cafe offer must be confirmed by host.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Common Press has an explicit literary and community identity, not just coffee with decorative books. The queer/intersectional curation gives a strong story and real event potential. Dalston branch is new, so operations need confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Time Out reported the beloved queer bookshop opening a second site on Stoke Newington Road in 2026.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Queer/intersectional books, events, community programming and cafe/bar identity.
venues.safety_notes: Stoke Newington Road is high-footfall; use Dalston Kingsland/Dalston Junction routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk by Google Maps search.
venues.risks_watchouts: New branch; cafe seating and group tolerance need direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Shoreditch/East address 118 Bethnal Green Road open Tue-Sun 11:00-18:30; about page lists Monday closed, Tue-Thu 11:00-18:30 and Sat-Sun 11:00-18:30. Friday hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop-cafe/bar open to public; group 8-12 walk-in seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible off-peak with heads-up; check events calendar.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop-cafe/community venue with events; seating may be event-dependent.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of London's clearest bookshop-cafe fits: books, coffee, queer community and events in one place. It is just outside the strict borough line but strongly serves Hackney/Shoreditch attendees. It is PAINTS-coded if the host manages capacity.
venues.anecdote_story: Bookshop.org describes it as London's queer intersectional bookshop and a sanctuary for storytelling, books and diverse queer events.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, cafe/bar service, author/community events, queer/intersectional shelves.
venues.safety_notes: Bethnal Green Road/Shoreditch High Street is busy; route to Shoreditch High Street station.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min transit; no same-category backup within 10 min in strict walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline district and event crowding; verify seating.

venues.opening_hours: Official site describes an Islington bookshop, wine bar and social space serving wine and coffee; Corner lists Friday/Saturday 10:00-21:00 updated Jun 2026. Exact weekly hours and group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public bookshop/cafe/wine bar; 8-12 walk-in group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible only with off-peak call; events may occupy space.
venues.capacity_notes: Designed as bookshop and social space, but outside Hackney proper.
venues.why_fits_paints: BookBar is a textbook Books & Coffee concept: books at the centre, coffee by day, wine/events around reading. It is included only as a Tier B geography stretch for north Hackney/Stoke Newington overflow. Use when Hackney bookshop-cafe options cannot hold the group.
venues.anecdote_story: BookBar says its mission is bringing people together through books; secondary coverage notes it launched in north London in 2021.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bookshop, wine bar, coffee, reading events and book-club culture.
venues.safety_notes: Blackstock Road is a main north London corridor; route via Arsenal/Finsbury Park buses or Highbury links.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min walk; closest PAINTS-coded alternative is The Common Press Dalston by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Geography stretch outside Hackney; keep Tier B.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Explicitly removed in the PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as backup even though it is an obvious Hackney walk.
PAINTS v6 marks Clissold Park as PAUSED; do not source until team review clears it.
Cemetery walks default not permitted under v6, and Abney is not the specific Highgate Cemetery exception. Also unsuitable as market/walk routing despite nearby venues.
Official site says Hackney Museum is closed for refurbishment and will reopen in 2027.
Strong pub candidate historically, but live sources conflict around 2025 closure and 2026 reopening; operational reliability needs a fresh host check before inclusion.
Beautiful De Beauvoir wine bar, but official policy says no reservations/private hire and no groups bigger than 6, below PAINTS 8-12 group requirement.
Good independent bookshop, but no verified on-site or literally next-door independent coffee/seating offer found, so it fails the raised Books & Coffee rule.
Excellent art/design book angle, but no verified cafe/coffee partner with 2-hour seating found; keep as a browsing stop, not a PAINTS Books & Coffee venue.
Interesting gallery, but official site says appointment-only/private viewing space with no public opening hours, failing walk-in robustness for PAINTS.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: London Fields as a Creative Walk
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not on the PAINTS v6 approved parks/routes list; can be a market-adjacent landmark but not a Creative Walk route.
